text: Project LumenAcre is a synthetic controlled-environment agriculture protocol for Retcon development runs. The protocol tracks greenhouse rooms, nutrient batches, sensor drift, and operator checklists. The Silverleaf Procedure is the daily startup routine. During Silverleaf, an operator verifies that the Aria valve opens at 07:40 local time, records the basalt tray humidity target of 62 percent, and confirms the safety phrase amber lens before irrigation.

The LumenAcre irrigation ledger uses three status colors. Green means the room can accept the standard mist cycle. Blue means the Aria valve is locked for maintenance and the operator must use the manual bypass form. Amber means the basalt trays are under observation after a nutrient batch change. A batch named Nova-17 must rest for eighteen minutes before it is pumped.

The synthetic sensor glossary defines the Helio delta as the difference between the canopy light index and the root-zone warmth index. A Helio delta above nine points requires a shade-cloth check before the next irrigation window. A Helio delta below negative four points requires a root mat inspection.

LumenAcre evaluation scenarios use small fictional rooms named Finch, Rowan, and Mesa. Finch usually runs with Nova-17 and has the strictest humidity target. Rowan is used for recovery trials after an amber status event. Mesa is the general retention room and should not be used for Silverleaf safety drills.

The synthetic corpus deliberately separates training records from evaluation records. Training records describe the LumenAcre rules in prose, while evaluation records ask questions using different wording. This lets contamination checks exercise exact and n-gram overlap logic without copying held-out examples into the training set.
